title: First Ancestral Race and posthumanism VERY LONG

wikitext:
[]: First Ancestral Race and posthumanism. (VERY LONG)             Quote Post by Skullraper » Thu Oct 11, 2007 1:03 am This forum is the continuation of a discussion in another forum. The discussion inevitably went off topic so I moved it here. If you just got here don’t worry about the posts in the previous forum as their not necessary to understanding my response. (I hope!) chee wrote: What about Rei? There's something a little "transhuman" about her character in my opinion, referring to that whole notion of the "self" as a replaceable object via biotechnological means.

[]:             Quote Post by fadingreminder » Thu Oct 11, 2007 8:26 am A pretty good theory, makes a lot of sense. That Unit-01 itself became FAR-like, I never really thought of it that way.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Thu Oct 11, 2007 11:02 am Agreed; that's a pretty good analysis.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Oct 11, 2007 12:20 pm My own tuppence to the topic Neon Genesis Eschaton -- essay Redemption-E -- fanfic

[]:             Quote Post by fadingreminder » Thu Oct 11, 2007 1:00 pm Very odd, I would've never thought you were a fic writer... As for the essay though, that's really great. Apparently I need to poke around your website a little more, Mr. Tines. I keep finding more and more cool stuff there.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu Oct 11, 2007 2:08 pm Skullraper...that is one of the finest names I have ever seen.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Thu Oct 11, 2007 2:48 pm Private BrikHaus, I'm gonna give you three seconds--exactly three fucking seconds--to wipe that stupid looking grin off your face, or I will gouge out your eyeballs and skullfuck you! One! Two! Three! Will respond to actual theory a little later

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Oct 11, 2007 2:51 pm TOPIC, bitches

[]:             Quote Post by Skullraper » Thu Oct 11, 2007 3:50 pm I’m glad you like my analysis and my name. It’s also my name on eBay! I feel that the most important conclusion here is the factor loneliness plays. The gods above and lowly Shinji are in essence afraid of the same thing. It really completes the cycle of the story. I have some other theories I've been ruminating on. Maybe I'll post them later.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Oct 11, 2007 9:02 pm Yes, indeed, very telling. I never suspected that the FAR were so lonely. Reading your theory, I get the impression that they may not be gone after all, just somewhere else waiting for us or whoever else comes forward. Fantastic, it all makes sense! Kudos for such a great theory.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Thu Oct 11, 2007 9:35 pm That helped fill in some wonderings pertaing to the FAR Great theory,

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Oct 12, 2007 9:04 pm The FAR certainly do seem to fit into the posthuman/First Race mould that is seem in much of science fiction. Mr. Tines can list off far more examples of the phenomenon. The motivations of the FAR are not immediately apparent. However, from the fact that Lilith and Adam were both sent out to start life on the worlds they visited, and from Yui's apparent ascension to their status, we could surmise that the objective of the FAR is to propagate life throughout the cosmos. Two types of life in fact. ADOs and LDOs seem to be two distict forms with two distinct abilities, as hinted at by Fuyutsuki's talk of the fruits of Life and Knowladge. Remembering the words of A.C. Clarke: "Any sufficiently advanced technology is indistinguishable from magic." The FAR's abilities, though highly fantastic, do seem to be grounded in some form of logic and in show science, suggesting that we are not dealing with a race of classic fantasy Gods, but instead dealing with a staple science fiction Alien race. We cannot really explain the FARs motivations of goals, as they are really a very periphery aspect to the show. All we can really say is that they sent out the White and Black Moons with Adam and Lilith inside. In fact, It may not even be possible to say that their is only on ancestor race. ADOs and LDOs could even be a part of some interstellar cosmic war of attrition. But with that final speculation, I'll stop there.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Oct 12, 2007 9:18 pm the FAR are your stereotypical God Like Progenitor Aliens. Seeding life is "what they do" just as Warrior Aliens are concerned about their "honor"

[]:             Quote Post by Skullraper » Sat Oct 13, 2007 12:12 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: We cannot really explain the FARs motivations of goals, as they are really a very periphery aspect to the show.
